project_type: C++ AI personal assistant OS (single compilation unit)
build_system: Makefile with clang++. Single compilation unit: mk_entry.cpp #includes all other .cpp files. No separate .o linking. Uses -lcurl -lsqlite3
test_framework: Custom test framework in tests/test_main.cpp using TEST_ASSERT macros and RUN_TEST. Tests compile as a separate single compilation unit that #includes module .cpp files directly.
verification_instructions: 1. Run 'make' from MK_OS/ - must compile with zero errors. 2. Run 'make test' - all tests must pass. 3. Verify the llm/ directory contains: llm_engine.cpp, model_manager.cpp, download_model.sh, start_server.sh, README.md
setup_instructions: No setup needed. clang++, libcurl, and libsqlite3 are already available.
environment_constraints: Linux (Amazon Linux 2023), clang++ 15, open internet access. No Docker daemon available for container builds. /proc/meminfo is available for RAM checks.
contribution_requirements: No formal CONTRIBUTING.md. Code follows MK OS patterns: #ifndef guards, classes prefixed with MK, Color:: namespace for terminal output, std::cout for status messages.
key_patterns: 1. All modules are header-like .cpp files with #ifndef guards, #included into mk_entry.cpp. 2. Classes use MK prefix (MKInputPreprocessor, MKSmartRouter, etc). 3. Boot sequence prints colored status lines with checkmarks. 4. HTTP calls use libcurl directly. 5. Tests #include module .cpp files directly. 6. Input preprocessor already has preprocessWithModel() that checks Ollama at localhost:11434.
directory_structure: MK_OS/ is root. system/ has mk_entry.cpp (main). ai_core/ has preprocessor, router, knowledge. network/ has HTTP. tools/ has standalone binaries. tests/ has test_main.cpp. mk_brain/ has reasoning/memory/embeddings modules.
